subject: Mathematics
q:
Consider the following frequency distribution:

Class: 0-6 6-12 12-18 18-24 24-30
Frequency: a b 12 9 5

If mean =30922 and median =14, then the value (a-b)2 is equal to
question:
Consider the following frequency distribution:

Class: 0-6 6-12 12-18 18-24 24-30
Frequency: a b 12 9 5

If mean =30922 and median =14, then the value (a-b)2 is equal to
solution:
Class Frequency  Mid-point xi fixi
0-6 a 0+62=3 3a
6-12 b 6+122=9 9b
12-18 12 12+182=15 180
18-24 9 18+242=21 189
24-30 5 24+302=27 135
  N=26+a+b   504+3a+9b

We know that, mean=∑fixi∑fi

Given, mean=30922

⇒3a+9 b+180+189+135a+b+26=30922

⇒504+3a+9 ba+b+26=30922

⇒66a+198 b+11088=309a+309 b+8034

⇒243a+111 b=3054

⇒81a+37b=1018   ...1

Now, median=14, which lies in the interval 12-18, thus 12-18 is the median class and we have =l+N2-c×hf,

Where, l is the lower limit of the median class i.e. l=12, c is the cumulative frequency of the class above the median class i.e. c=a+b, f is the frequency of the median class i.e. f=12 and h is the length of the interval i.e. h=6.

Thus, we have 12+a+b+262-(a+b)12×6=14

⇒a+b+26-2a-2b22=14-12

⇒26-a-b4=2

⇒26-a-b=8

⇒a+b=18   ...2

On solving the equations 1 and 2, we get a=8, b=10.

∴ a-b2=8-102=4.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the mean and the standard deviation of the data <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> respectively, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> are the roots of the equation:
question: If the mean and the standard deviation of the data <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> respectively, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> are the roots of the equation:
solution:
5+3+7+a+b=25⇒a+b=10
S.D. =52+32+72+a2+b25-52=2
=a2+b2+835-25=4⇒a2+b2=62
⇒(a+b)2-2ab=62⇒ab=19
so equation whose roots are a and b is x2-10x+19=0

subject: Mathematics
q: For $10$ observations $x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{10}$, if $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i+2)^2=180$ and $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i-1)^2=90$, then their standard deviation is:
question: For $10$ observations $x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{10}$, if $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i+2)^2=180$ and $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i-1)^2=90$, then their standard deviation is:
solution:
Given $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i+2)^2=180$

$\Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 + 4\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i + 40 = 180$

$\Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 + 4\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i = 140$

Also given $\sum_{i=1}^{10}(x_i-1)^2=90$

$\Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 - 2\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i + 10 = 90$

$\Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 - 2\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i = 80$

Subtracting the second equation from the first:

$6\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i = 60 \Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i = 10$

Substituting this value in the second equation:

$\sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 - 2(10) = 80 \Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{10} x_i^2 = 100$

The standard deviation is given by:

$\sigma = \sqrt{\dfrac{\sum x_i^2}{n} - \left(\dfrac{\sum x_i}{n}\right)^2}$

$\sigma = \sqrt{\dfrac{100}{10} - \left(\dfrac{10}{10}\right)^2}$

$\sigma = \sqrt{10 - 1} = \sqrt{9} = 3$

Answer: $3$

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of five observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9.20</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If three of the given five observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then a ratio of other two observations is
question: The mean of five observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9.20</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If three of the given five observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then a ratio of other two observations is
solution:
Let, the two unknown terms are x & y.

The mean is defined as μ=∑xiN

⇒5=1+3+8+x+y5

⇒25=12+x+y

⇒x+y=13    ...1

And, the variance is defined as σ2=∑xi-μ2N=∑xi2N-μ2

⇒9.2=1+9+64+x2+y25-25

⇒34.2×5=74+x2+y2

⇒171=74+x2+y2

⇒97=x2+y2     ...2

Put the value of y from equation 1 and put in 2, to get

97=x2+13-x2

⇒97=x2+169-26x+x2

⇒2x2-26x+72=0

⇒x2-13x+36=0

⇒x-4x-9=0

⇒x=4 or x=9

And, y=9 or y=4 respectively.

Hence, the ratio of x : y is 4 : 9 or 9 : 4.

q: If the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> natural numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>k</mi></math> is less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the maximum possible value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> is ___________.
question: If the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> natural numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>k</mi></math> is less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the maximum possible value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> is ___________.
solution:
σ2=Σx2n-Σxn2

=9+k210-9+k102<10

90+10k2-81-k2-18k<1000

9k2-18k-991<0

k2-2k<9919

k-12<10009

-10103<k-1<10103

k<10103+1

k≤11

Maximum value of k is 11.

subject: Mathematics
q: From a lot of 10 items, which include 3 defective items, a sample of 5 items is drawn at random. Let the random variable $\mathrm{X}$ denote the number of defective items in the sample. If the variance of $\mathrm{X}$ is $\sigma^2$, then $96 \sigma^2$ is equal to ______
question: From a lot of 10 items, which include 3 defective items, a sample of 5 items is drawn at random. Let the random variable $\mathrm{X}$ denote the number of defective items in the sample. If the variance of $\mathrm{X}$ is $\sigma^2$, then $96 \sigma^2$ is equal to ______
solution:
$X=$ denotes number of defective
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{x} & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x}) & \frac{7}{15} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{1}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & 1 & 4 & 9 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{20}{12} & \frac{9}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}} & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{10}{12} & \frac{3}{12} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\mu=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{18}{12}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2=\frac{34}{12} \\
& \sigma^2=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2-(\mu)^2
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{34}{12}-\left(\frac{18}{12}\right)^2=\frac{17}{6}-\frac{9}{4} \\ & \frac{34-27}{12}=\frac{7}{12} \\ & 96 \sigma^2=96 \times \frac{7}{12}=56\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean and variance of a set of 15 numbers are 12 and 14 respectively. The mean and variance of another set of 15 numbers are 14 and σ2 respectively. If the variance of all the 30 numbers in the two sets is 13, then σ2 is equal to
question: The mean and variance of a set of 15 numbers are 12 and 14 respectively. The mean and variance of another set of 15 numbers are 14 and σ2 respectively. If the variance of all the 30 numbers in the two sets is 13, then σ2 is equal to
solution:
Given that,

For set 1:

Mean =12 and Variance =14

For set 2:

Mean =14 and Variance =σ2

Now, For set 1 we have,

⇒∑i=1i=15xi15=12

⇒∑i=1i=15xi=15×12

Also, ∑i=1i=15xi215-∑i=1i=15xi152=14

 ⇒∑i=1i=15xi215-122=14

⇒∑i=1i=15xi2=14+144×15

Now for set 2:

⇒∑i=1i=15yi15=14

and ⇒∑i=1i=15yi=15×14

Also, ∑i=1i=15yi215-∑i=1i=15yi152=σ2

⇒∑i=1i=15yi215-142=σ2

⇒∑i=1i=15yi2=σ2+196×15

Now, 

The combined variance will be

​​​​​​⇒13=∑i=1i=15xi2×15+∑i=1i=15yi2×1515+15-∑i=1i=15xi+∑i=1i=15yi15+152

⇒13=14+144×15+σ2+196×1530-132

∴σ2=10

Hence, σ2=10

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> is the mean deviation of the numbers about the mean, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mi>M</mi></math> is equal to
question: The mean of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> is the mean deviation of the numbers about the mean, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mi>M</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Mean x¯=a+6+8+5+105=6 (given) 

⇒a+b=7     ...i

Variance =∑xi2n-x¯2=a2+b2+82+52+1025-62=6.8 (given)

⇒a2+b2=25     ...ii

on solving equations i & ii, we get a=3, b=4

Now, Mean deviation about Mean M=∑xi-x¯n

⇒3-6+4-6+8-6+5-6+10-65

⇒ 3+2+2+1+45=125

∴  M=125⇒25M=60.

subject: Mathematics
q: Suppose that the mean and median of the non-negative numbers $21, 8, 17, a, 51, 103, b, 13, 67, (a > b)$, are $40$ and $21$, respectively. If the mean deviation about the median is $26$, then $2a$ is equal to:
question: Suppose that the mean and median of the non-negative numbers $21, 8, 17, a, 51, 103, b, 13, 67, (a > b)$, are $40$ and $21$, respectively. If the mean deviation about the median is $26$, then $2a$ is equal to:
solution:
The given numbers are $21, 8, 17, a, 51, 103, b, 13, 67$. There are $9$ numbers in total.

Given that the mean is $40$, the sum of the numbers is:
$\sum x_i = 9 \times 40 = 360$

The sum of the known numbers is:
$21 + 8 + 17 + 51 + 103 + 13 + 67 = 280$

Therefore, the sum of $a$ and $b$ is:
$a + b = 360 - 280 = 80$

The median of the $9$ numbers is $21$. Arranging the known numbers in ascending order, we get:
$8, 13, 17, 21, 51, 67, 103$

There are $3$ numbers less than $21$ and $3$ numbers greater than $21$. For $21$ to be the median (the $5^{\text{th}}$ observation), one of the unknown numbers must be $\le 21$ and the other must be $\ge 21$. Since $a > b$, we must have:
$a \ge 21$ and $b \le 21$

The mean deviation about the median is $26$, so the sum of absolute deviations from the median is:
$\sum |x_i - 21| = 9 \times 26 = 234$

The sum of absolute deviations of the known numbers from $21$ is:
$|8 - 21| + |13 - 21| + |17 - 21| + |21 - 21| + |51 - 21| + |67 - 21| + |103 - 21|$
$= 13 + 8 + 4 + 0 + 30 + 46 + 82 = 183$

The sum of absolute deviations of $a$ and $b$ from $21$ is:
$|a - 21| + |b - 21| = 234 - 183 = 51$

Since $a \ge 21$ and $b \le 21$, we can remove the absolute values:
$(a - 21) + (21 - b) = 51$
$a - b = 51$

We now have a system of two equations:
$a + b = 80$
$a - b = 51$

Adding the two equations, we get:
$2a = 131$

Answer: $131$

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let μ  be the mean and σ be the standard deviation of the distribution

Xi 0 1 2 3 4 5
fi k+2 2k k2-1 k2-1 k2+1 k-3

where Σfi=62. If x denotes the greatest integer ≤x, thenμ2 + σ2  is equal to
question:
Let μ  be the mean and σ be the standard deviation of the distribution

Xi 0 1 2 3 4 5
fi k+2 2k k2-1 k2-1 k2+1 k-3

where Σfi=62. If x denotes the greatest integer ≤x, thenμ2 + σ2  is equal to
solution:
Given,

Xi 0 1 2 3 4 5
fi k+2 2k k2-1 k2-1 k2+1 k-3

Now we know that,

Mean=∑Xifi∑fi

⇒μ=2k+ 2k2 –2 + 3k2–3 + 4k2+4 +5k – 1562

⇒μ=9k2+7k-1662 .......1

And ∑fi=62

⇒3k2+4k-2=62

⇒3k2+4k-64=0

⇒(3k+16)(k-4)=0

⇒k=4

Now putting the value of k in above equation 1 we get,

μ=15662

Now finding variance we get,

σ2=∑Xi2fi∑fi-Mean2

⇒σ2=1·2k+4k2-1+9k2-1+16k2+1+25k-362-156622

⇒σ2=29k2+27k-7262-μ2

Now putting the value of k in above equation we get,

⇒σ2=50062-μ2

⇒σ2+μ2=50062

⇒σ2+μ2=8

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo></math> respectively. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> of these observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>14</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>15</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the absolute difference of the remaining two observations is :
question: The mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo></math> respectively. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> of these observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>14</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>15</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the absolute difference of the remaining two observations is :
solution:
Let remaining two observations are a and b

Σxin=5+7+12+10+15+14+a+b8=10

⇒ 63+a+b=80

⇒ a+b=17

σ2=Σxi2n−Σxin2

⇒13.5=25+49+144+100+225+196+a2+b28−100

⇒908=a2+b2+739

⇒a2+b2=169

⇒a+b2−2ab=169

⇒289−169=2ab

⇒ ab=60

∴ a−b2=a+b2−4ab=289−240=49

∴ a−b=7.

subject: Mathematics
q:
If the mean of the following probability distribution of a random variable $\mathrm{X}$ :
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{X} & 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 & 8 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{X}) & a & 2 a & a+b & 2 b & 3 b \\
\hline
\end{array}$
is $\frac{46}{9}$, then the variance of the distribution is
question:
If the mean of the following probability distribution of a random variable $\mathrm{X}$ :
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{X} & 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 & 8 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{X}) & a & 2 a & a+b & 2 b & 3 b \\
\hline
\end{array}$
is $\frac{46}{9}$, then the variance of the distribution is
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \sum P_i=1 \\ & a+2 a+a+b+2 b+3 b=1\end{aligned}$ $4 a+6 b=1$ ...(I) $\mathrm{E}(\mathrm{x})=$ mean $=\frac{46}{9}$ $\begin{aligned} & \sum P_i X_i=\frac{46}{9} \Rightarrow 4 a+4 a+4 b+12 b+24 b=\frac{46}{9} \\ & 8 a+40 b=\frac{46}{9}\end{aligned}$ $4 a+20 b=\frac{23}{9}$ ...(II) Subtract (I) from (II) we get $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{b}=\frac{1}{9} \& \mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{12} \\ & \text { Variance }=\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}{ }^2\right)-\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2=0^2 \times 9^2+2^2 \times 2 \mathrm{a}+4^2(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})+6^2(2 \mathrm{~b})+8^2(3 \mathrm{~b}) \\ & =24 \mathrm{a}+280 \mathrm{~b} \end{aligned}$
Put $\mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{12} \quad \mathrm{~b}=\frac{1}{9}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}^2\right)=2+\frac{280}{9}=\frac{298}{9} \\ & \therefore \sigma^2=\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}^2\right)-\mathrm{E}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{298}{9}-\left(\frac{46}{9}\right)^2 \\ & \sigma^2=\frac{298}{9}-\frac{2116}{81} \\ & =\frac{566}{81} \end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the mean and variance of the following data: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>13</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>37</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> respectively, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to:
question: If the mean and variance of the following data: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>13</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>37</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> respectively, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to:
solution:
Mean =6+10+7+13+a+12+b+128=9

⇒60+a+b=72

⇒a+b=12    ...1

variance =∑xi2n-∑xin2=374

Here, Σxi2=62+102+72+132+a2+b2+122+122

=a2+b2+642

So, variance is a2+b2+6428-92=374

⇒a2+b28+3214-81=374

⇒a2+b28=81+374-3214

⇒a2+b28=81-71

∴ a2+b2=80

From 1 a2+b2+2ab=144

⇒80+2ab=144  ∴ 2ab=64

So, a-b2=a2+b2-2ab=80-64=16.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let the positive numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></mrow></msub><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> be in a G.P. Let their mean and variance be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>31</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math> respectively, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> are co-prime. If the mean of their reciprocals is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>31</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>14</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to ____________.
question: Let the positive numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></mrow></msub><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> be in a G.P. Let their mean and variance be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>31</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math> respectively, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> are co-prime. If the mean of their reciprocals is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>31</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>14</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to ____________.
solution:
5 positive numbers a1, a2,..., a5 are in geometric progression. So, let the numbers be ar2, ar, a, ar, ar2

According to question,

ar2+ar+a+ar+ar25=3110   ...1

And,

r2a+ra+1a+1ar+1ar25=3140

⇒1ar2+r+1+1r+1r25=3140   ...2

By 1 & 2, we get

a2=4 ⇒a=2

So,

r2+r+1+1r+1r2=314

⇒r+1r2+r+1r=274+2

⇒t2+t=354

where, t=r+1r

4t2+4t-35=0

⇒t=52⇒r=2

So, numbers are 12, 1, 2, 4, 8

Variance is

=14+1+4+16+645-12+1+2+4+852

=18625=mn

m+n=211

Hence this is the required option.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The frequency distribution of the age of students in a class of 40 students is given below.
$\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|l|l|}
\hline \text{Age} & 15 & 16 & 17 & 18 & 19 & 20 \\
\hline \text{No of Students} & 5 & 8 & 5 & 12 & x & y \\
\hline
\end{array}$
If the mean deviation about the median is 1.25, then $4 x+5 y$ is equal to :
question:
The frequency distribution of the age of students in a class of 40 students is given below.
$\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|l|l|}
\hline \text{Age} & 15 & 16 & 17 & 18 & 19 & 20 \\
\hline \text{No of Students} & 5 & 8 & 5 & 12 & x & y \\
\hline
\end{array}$
If the mean deviation about the median is 1.25, then $4 x+5 y$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=10 \ldots \ldots \ldots .(1) \\ & \text {Median }=18=\mathrm{M} \\ & \text {M.D. }=\frac{\sum \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{i}}\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{M}\right|}{\sum \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{i}}} \\ & 1.25=\frac{36+\mathrm{x}+2 \mathrm{y}}{40} \\ & \mathrm{x}+2 \mathrm{y}=14 \ldots \ldots \ldots .(2) \\ & \text { by (1) & (2) } \\ & \mathrm{x}=6, \mathrm{y}=4 \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \mathrm{x}+5 \mathrm{y}=24+20=44\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|}
\hline \operatorname{Age}\left(\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}\right) & \mathrm{f} & \left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{M}\right| & \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{i}}\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{M}\right| \\
\hline 15 & 5 & 3 & 15 \\
\hline 16 & 8 & 2 & 16 \\
\hline 17 & 5 & 1 & 5 \\
\hline 18 & 12 & 0 & 0 \\
\hline 19 & \mathrm{x} & 1 & \mathrm{x} \\
\hline 20 & \mathrm{y} & 2 & 2 \mathrm{y} \\
\hline
\end{array}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If both the mean and the standard deviation of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub></math> are equal to <math><mn>16</mn></math>, then the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
question: If both the mean and the standard deviation of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub></math> are equal to <math><mn>16</mn></math>, then the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
solution:
For observations x1, x2, ...........x50

We have, mean, x¯=∑xi 50=16   .....i

And, standard deviation, σ=∑xi250-x¯2=16

⇒∑xi250=162+x¯2

⇒∑xi250=162+162=512  .....ii

Now, the mean value of x1-42, x2-42, ..., x50-42 will be

=∑xi-4250=∑xi2-8 ∑xi+16×5050

=∑xi250-8∑xi50+16

 Using i and ii,

=512-8×16+16=400.

subject: Mathematics
q: Consider a set of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers having variance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo></math> In this set, the mean of first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and the mean of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> numbers is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> A new set is constructed by adding <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> into each of the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers, and subtracting <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> from each of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> numbers. If the variance of the new set is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to ______.
question: Consider a set of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers having variance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo></math> In this set, the mean of first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and the mean of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> numbers is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> A new set is constructed by adding <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> into each of the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> numbers, and subtracting <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> from each of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> numbers. If the variance of the new set is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to ______.
solution:
Let, the number be a1, a2, a3,..., a2n, b1, b2, b3,..., bn.

Given, the mean of the first 2n numbers is 6 and that of the remaining n numbers is 3, then by using the formula for combined mean, we get, the mean of the complete set as μ=2n×6+n×32n+n=15n3n=5.

Also, the variance is defined as σ2=∑xi2n-μ2

4=∑ai2+∑bi23n-(5)2

⇒∑ai2+∑bi23n=29

⇒∑ai2+∑bi2=87n   ...i

Now, as per the given information, the distribution becomes

a1+1, a2+1, a3+1,..., a2n+1, b1-1, b2-1,..., bn-1

Thus, the new variance is

k=∑(ai+1)2+∑(bi-1)23n-12n+2n+3n-n3n2

⇒k=∑ai2+2n+2∑ai+∑bi2+n-2∑bi3n-1632

⇒k=∑ai2+∑bi2+3n+2∑ai-2∑bi3n-1632

On putting the given values and value from the equation i, 

⇒k=87n+3n+2(12n)-2(3n)3n-1632

⇒k=1083-1632

⇒9k=3(108)-(16)2

⇒9k=324-256=68.

subject: Mathematics
q: In an experiment with 15 observations on $x$, the following results were available: $\Sigma x^2=2830, \Sigma x=170$ One observation that was 20 was found to be wrong and was replaced by the correct value 30 . The corrected variance is
question: In an experiment with 15 observations on $x$, the following results were available: $\Sigma x^2=2830, \Sigma x=170$ One observation that was 20 was found to be wrong and was replaced by the correct value 30 . The corrected variance is
solution:
$\Sigma x=170, \Sigma x^2=2830$ increase in $\Sigma x=10$, then
$\Sigma x^{\prime}=170+10=180$
Increase in $\Sigma \mathrm{x}^2=900-400=500$ then
$\Sigma x^{\prime 2}=2830+500=3330$
Variance $=\frac{1}{n} \Sigma x^{\prime 2}-\left(\frac{1}{n} \Sigma x^{\prime}\right)^2$
$=\frac{1}{15} \times 3330-\left(\frac{1}{15} \times 180\right)^2=222-144=78$

q:
Consider the following statements:
Mode can be computed from histogram
Median is not independent of change of scale
Variance is independent of change of origin and scale.
question:
Consider the following statements:
Mode can be computed from histogram
Median is not independent of change of scale
Variance is independent of change of origin and scale.
solution: Mode can be computed from histogram and median is dependent on the scale. Hence statement (a) and (b) are correct

subject: Mathematics
q: If the mean deviation about the mean of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is odd, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to ______.
question: If the mean deviation about the mean of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is odd, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to ______.
solution:
We know that mean deviation about the mean of odd natural number is given by M.D x=1nn2-14

So, now 1nn2-14=5n+1n (given)

⇒n-1=20⇒n=21

q: Let the mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> observations be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>9</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> respectively. Later on, it was observed that two observations were considered as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> instead of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn></math> respectively. If the correct variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> are coprime, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> are coprime, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to
question: Let the mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> observations be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>9</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> respectively. Later on, it was observed that two observations were considered as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> instead of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn></math> respectively. If the correct variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> are coprime, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> are coprime, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

The mean and variance of 12 observations be 92 and 4 respectively,

So, mean will be,

 x¯=x1+x2+……+9+10+…..+x1212=92

⇒x1+x2……+x12+19=54

Now removing the observation 9 & 10 and adding the observation 7 & 14,

So, the new total sum of the observation will be,

x1+x2+……+x12+7+14=54-19+7+14

Now new mean will be,

x1+x2+….+x12+7+1412=5612

⇒x¯new=143
Now using the formula of the variance we get,

x12+x22+……+x122+92+10212-922=4

⇒x12+x22+……+x122+92+102-81×3=4×12

⇒x12+x22+……+x122+92+102=291

Now removing 92 & 102 and adding 72 & 142 we get,

⇒x12+x22+……+x122+72+142=355
New variance =∑xi2N-(x¯)2
=35512-1432
=28136

Now on comparing with mn=28136 we get,

⇒m=281 and n=36

∴ m+n=317

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given data can also be expressed as:

xi152535fi2x2

So,

x¯=∑fixiΣfi=30+25x+704+x=254+x4+x=25

Now, σ2=50=∑fixi2Σfi-x¯2

50=450+625x+24504+x-252

⇒50=2900+625x4+x-625

⇒675(4+x)=2900+625x

⇒50x=200⇒x=4

q: If the variance of the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> natural numbers is <math><mn>10</mn></math> and the variance of the first <math><mi>m</mi></math> even natural numbers is <math><mn>16</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> then the value of  <math><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to
question: If the variance of the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> natural numbers is <math><mn>10</mn></math> and the variance of the first <math><mi>m</mi></math> even natural numbers is <math><mn>16</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi></math> then the value of  <math><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
var1,2,…n=10⇒12+22+…+n2n-1+2+…+nn2=10

⇒n+12n+16-n+122=10

⇒n2-1=120⇒n=11

var2,4,6….,2m=16⇒var1,2….,m=4

⇒m2-1=48⇒m=7⇒m+n=18

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let the mean of the data

x 1 3 5 7 9
Frequency f 4 24 28 α 8

be 5. If m and σ2 are respectively the mean deviation about the mean and the variance of the data, then 3αm+σ2 is equal to _______.
question:
Let the mean of the data

x 1 3 5 7 9
Frequency f 4 24 28 α 8

be 5. If m and σ2 are respectively the mean deviation about the mean and the variance of the data, then 3αm+σ2 is equal to _______.
solution:
Given,

The mean of the below data is 5

x 1 3 5 7 9
Frequency f 4 24 28 α 8

Now finding mean we get,

4+72+28×5+7α+7264+α=5

⇒α=16

So, total number of observation will be, ∑fi=80

Now finding, mean deviation we get,

M.D=∑fixi-5∑fi

=4×4+24×2+0+16×2+8×480=85

So, m=85

Now finding, variance we get, 

σ2=∑fixi-52∑fi

⇒σ2=4×16+24×4+0+16×4+8×1680

⇒σ2=225

Hence, the value of 3αm+σ2=3×1685+225=8

subject: Mathematics
q: In a set of $2 n$ observations, half of them are equal to ' $\mathrm{a}$ ' and the remaining hall are equal to ' $-\mathrm{a}^{\prime}$ '. If the standard deviation of all the observations is 2 ; then the value of $|a|$ is :
question: In a set of $2 n$ observations, half of them are equal to ' $\mathrm{a}$ ' and the remaining hall are equal to ' $-\mathrm{a}^{\prime}$ '. If the standard deviation of all the observations is 2 ; then the value of $|a|$ is :
solution:
Clearly mean $\mathrm{A}=0$
Now, standard deviation $\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{\Sigma(x-\mathrm{A})^2}{2 n}}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
2=& \sqrt{\frac{(a-0)^2+(a-0)^2+\ldots \ldots+(0-a)^2+\ldots . .}{2 n}} \\
&=\sqrt{\frac{a^2 \cdot 2 n}{2 n}}=|a|
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, $|a|=2$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the mean deviation about median for the number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>16</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>21</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>24</mn></math> arranged in the ascending order, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> then the median is
question: If the mean deviation about median for the number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>16</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>21</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>24</mn></math> arranged in the ascending order, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> then the median is
solution:
Median is the central value (or middle observation) of statistical data if it is arranged in ascending or descending order.

Thus, if there are n observations (variates) x1, x2, x3, …, xn arranged in ascending or descending order, then

median =n+12th observation, if n is odd

and median =n2 th observation +n2+1 th observation 2, if n is even.

Here we can see there are even number of terms,

So, median =2k+122=k+6

Mean deviation =∑xi-Mn=6

⇒k+3+k+1+k-1+6-k+6-k+10-k+15-k+18-k8=6

⇒ 58-2k8=6

⇒k=5

So, median =2×5+122=11

subject: Mathematics
q: A student scores the following marks in five tests: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45,54,41,57,43</mn></math>. His score is not known for the sixth test. If the mean score is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>48</mn></math> in the six tests, then the standard deviation of the marks in six tests is:
question: A student scores the following marks in five tests: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45,54,41,57,43</mn></math>. His score is not known for the sixth test. If the mean score is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>48</mn></math> in the six tests, then the standard deviation of the marks in six tests is:
solution:
Given mean of 6 observations is 48

⇒ 41+45+54+57+43+x6=48

⇒x=48
Now variance σ2=∑x-x2n

=72+32+62+92+52+026
=1003
Hence standard deviation =103.

subject: Mathematics
q: A data consists of <math><mi>n</mi></math> observations: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>…</mo><mo> </mo><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>n</mi></munderover><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mi>n</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo stretchy="true">∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>n</mi></munderover><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>n</mi></math>, then the standard deviation of this data is
question: A data consists of <math><mi>n</mi></math> observations: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mo>…</mo><mo> </mo><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>n</mi></munderover><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mi>n</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo stretchy="true">∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>n</mi></munderover><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>n</mi></math>, then the standard deviation of this data is
solution:
Given:

∑i=1nxi+12=9n

⇒∑i=1nxi2+2xi+1=9n  ...1

And, ∑i=1nxi-12=5n

⇒∑i=1nxi2-2xi+1=5n  ...2 

Form 1+2, we get

∑i=1n2xi2+2=14n 

⇒∑i=1nxi2=6n  ...3 

From 1-2, we get 

⇒∑i=1nxi=n  ...4

∴ Variance, σ2=Ex2-Ex2

⇒σ2=∑x2n-∑xn 

⇒σ2=6nn-nn2=6-1=5

∴ Standard deviation, σ=5.

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> teachers in a school is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> years. A teacher retires at the age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn></math> years and a new teacher is appointed in his place. If the mean age of the teachers in this school now is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>39</mn></math> years, then the age (in years) of the newly appointed teacher is
question: The mean age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> teachers in a school is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> years. A teacher retires at the age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn></math> years and a new teacher is appointed in his place. If the mean age of the teachers in this school now is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>39</mn></math> years, then the age (in years) of the newly appointed teacher is
solution:
Mean age can be calculated as

x-=x1+x2+…x2525=40

⇒ x1+x2+…+x25=1000

Let age of new teacher be A, then

1000-60+A=39×25=975

⇒ A=975-940=35

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> observations were calculated as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> respectively by a student who took by mistake <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> instead of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> for one observation. Then, the correct standard deviation is _______.
question: The mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> observations were calculated as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> respectively by a student who took by mistake <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> instead of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> for one observation. Then, the correct standard deviation is _______.
solution:
Given,

n=10 and mean x¯=∑xi10=15

⇒∑i=110xi=150

⇒∑i=19xi+25=150

⇒∑i=19xi=125

Now new sum will be ∑i=19xi+15=140

So, actual mean =14010=14=x¯new 

Now finding variance 

∑xi210-x¯2=15

⇒∑xi210-152=15

⇒∑i=110xi2=2400

Removing observation of 25 we get,

⇒∑i=19xi2+625=2400

⇒∑i=19xi2=1775

Now adding observation 15 we get,

∑i=19xi2+152=2000=∑xi2actual 

Now calculating actual standard deviation we get,

σactual 2=∑xi2actual 10-x¯new 2

=200010-142

=200-196=4

Calculating standard deviation, S.Dactual =4=2
